title: Mahabalipuram
description: Mahabalipuram, also known as Mamallapuram, is a historic town in Tamil Nadu, India, renowned for its 7th and 8th-century rock-cut temples and monolithic sculptures. As a UNESCO World Heritage site, it serves as an open-air museum featuring the famous Shore Temple, the Five Rathas, and the massive Arjuna's Penance bas-relief. The town is famous for its intricate Dravidian architecture, pristine beaches, and vibrant handicraft industry, making it a perfect blend of ancient history and coastal relaxation.
hotels: Radisson Blu Resort Temple Bay Mamallapuram
travel_methods: The most convenient way to reach Mahabalipuram is by flying into Chennai International Airport (MAA), which is about 55 km away, followed by a taxi or bus ride. By train, Chengalpattu Junction is the nearest major railway station, approximately 30 km away. Regular buses run from Chennai (CMBT or Tiruvanmiyur) directly to the town.
